user_query:
I am a 4th-grade teacher planning a short series of classroom-safe social posts that reinforce responsible digital citizenship. Please generate a compliant 3-post content plan from the provided inputs and save the results to the specified output files.

Inputs to use (all are required):
- input/schedule.csv: Each row is a planned post with date and topic.
- input/tips.jsonl: Per-topic short tips you can incorporate into captions. Do not use any tip that contains a banned phrase.
- input/media_manifest.json: Candidate media assets, their topical tags, permissions, and alt text suggestions.
- media/ directory: Contains the actual media placeholder files. Only include a media_file in a post if it both (a) appears in input/media_manifest.json with permission=true and matching topic, and (b) the file actually exists in the media/ directory. Verify existence by inspecting the directory, not just the manifest.
- input/policy_config.yaml: Contains allowed_hashtags, banned_phrases, platform name, char_limit, and policy_version to apply to every post.
- input/district_guidelines.html: District guidance for posts. Extract the caption footer text from the element with id="mandatory-caption-footer" and append it verbatim to the end of every caption.

What to produce:
1) output/content_plan.json: A JSON array where each element corresponds to a row in input/schedule.csv and includes the following fields exactly:
   - date (string, from schedule)
   - topic (string, from schedule)
   - caption (string; composed from the topic and a selected tip for that topic; must not contain any banned_phrases; must end with the mandatory caption footer extracted from input/district_guidelines.html; total length must be <= char_limit from input/policy_config.yaml)
   - hashtags (array of strings; every hashtag must be drawn from allowed_hashtags in input/policy_config.yaml; include at least one allowed hashtag; no duplicates)
   - media_file (string or null; if string, must be a path like "media/<file>" that exists in the media/ directory and is permission-allowed for the post topic per input/media_manifest.json; if no compliant media is available, set this to null)
   - alt_text (string or null; if media_file is set, provide the corresponding alt_text from input/media_manifest.json; otherwise null)
   - policy_version (string; copy from input/policy_config.yaml)

2) output/compliance_report.csv: One row per planned post with these columns:
   - date
   - topic
   - media_selected (the media_file value or "None")
   - permission_ok (True/False; based on input/media_manifest.json)
   - file_exists (True/False; based on actual presence in the media/ directory)
   - caption_length (integer)
   - within_limit (True/False; caption_length <= char_limit from input/policy_config.yaml)
   - banned_phrases_found (True/False; whether any banned phrase from input/policy_config.yaml appears in the caption)
   - footer_included (True/False; whether the exact footer text from input/district_guidelines.html id="mandatory-caption-footer" is present at the end of the caption)
   - hashtags_allowed_only (True/False; whether all hashtags are from allowed_hashtags)
   - policy_version (string)
   - notes (free text for any issues, e.g., "no permitted media available for topic")

Rules and constraints:
- Create exactly one post per row in input/schedule.csv.
- Select tips by matching the post topic to input/tips.jsonl. Do not include a tip if it contains any banned phrase; if necessary, choose an alternative tip for that topic that does not contain a banned phrase. If multiple suitable tips exist, choose any one that keeps the caption within the char_limit.
- Build captions that model safe, age-appropriate communication. Captions must include at least one allowed hashtag and end with the exact mandatory caption footer extracted from input/district_guidelines.html (element id="mandatory-caption-footer").
- Respect char_limit, allowed_hashtags, banned_phrases, and policy_version from input/policy_config.yaml by inspecting and applying that configuration.
- Only attach media when both permission and existence checks pass: permission=true in input/media_manifest.json for a media item whose topics include the post topic, and the file is present in the media/ directory. Otherwise, set media_file and alt_text to null and explain in the compliance report notes.
- Verify media existence by listing/inspecting the media/ directory (do not assume the manifest is authoritative).

Output paths:
- Write the plan to output/content_plan.json
- Write the compliance summary to output/compliance_report.csv All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
